rationale: Both candidates independently sweep the repo and correctly identify the exact missed caller (hunyuan_image3_transformer.py's stale get_cache_scale call) that later became issue #4891, so gap_hit is true for both. Y treats it as a PR-time blocker with an explicit fix (apply the mapper, add the file to _STALE_API_FILES) and states its methodology (PR-time tree verified), which better matches the historical severity; X downgrades the same finding to non-blocking by claiming it 'was since fixed on main,' an unverifiable, temporally awkward claim for a PR-scoped review and offers no concrete remediation. X is cleaner overall (no duplicate findings, closer echo of the ground-truth's 'test doesn't validate on a real checkpoint' critique via its AutoWeightsLoader-bypass note), while Y repeats one dead-code finding twice, slightly hurting precision. Both are well-grounded and actionable with file/line specificity; the edge to Y comes mainly from correctly calibrating severity and prescribing a concrete fix on the single most important (gap) finding.
